purpose: Evaluates whether the current maturity wall will produce systemic stress or be defused, incorporating structural differences from four prior episodes that were all resolved without crisis.
components: Base rate: 0-for-4 prior maturity walls produced systemic stress
Structural differentiator: CLO reinvestment expiration ($250-350B over 18 months)
CLO three-way squeeze: reinvestment expiration + arbitrage deterioration + CCC migration thresholds
Refinancing cost premium: 40-60% higher than prior episodes
A&E forbearance: $200-400B pushed out 2-3 years with PIK compounding at 10-13%
Falsification: SOFR decline 100-150bp restoring CLO reset economics
Key real-time variable: CCC bucket utilization (4% vs 6.5% toward 7.5% threshold)
current_assessment: Current episode structurally different from prior four via concurrent CLO demand withdrawal and higher refinancing costs. Bear case has force but 0-for-4 base rate cannot be dismissed with n=4 in non-stationary environment. Monitor CLO reset activity, SOFR path, and CCC bucket utilization.
limitations: n=4 in non-stationary environment insufficient for statistical confidence
CLO CCC bucket utilization real-time data availability limited
Interaction between CLO expiration and fiscal buffer unmodeled
